page: Microsoft Azure A2m_v2 is an Av2-family virtual machine configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 2 physical cores utilizing the x86_64 architecture. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8171M processor running at 2.6 GHz, the server features 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The hardware configuration includes a 35 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache, alongside 20 GB of bundled local storage. It operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and does not include a GPU or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations place the performance of this server type consistently in the bottom 10% across all tested categories. The Intel Xeon 8171M processor achieved a stress-ng single-core score of 344.35 and a multi-core score of 670.13, while the Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores registered at 364.0 and 657.0, respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show 60.64 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 6.81 GB/sec for large-block reads and 6.18 GB/sec for large-block writes. Cryptographic and compression capabilities are also limited, with a Passmark CPU encryption score of 303.59 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 7.31 GB/sec.

The primary tradeoff of the A2m_v2 is its low computational and memory throughput relative to its memory capacity. While it offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8, the underlying hardware delivers low processing efficiency, as evidenced by a Passmark database operations score of 441.64 and static web serving throughput of 0.89 GB/sec. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this instance serves as a low-cost entry point for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high CPU performance. It is appropriate for lightweight database operations, basic caching layers, or small-scale memory-bound applications where processing speed is not a critical constraint.
description: Microsoft Azure A2m_v2 is an Av2-family virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per core memory-to-CPU ratio. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8171M processor running at 2.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this server includes 20 GB of bundled local storage and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmark data indicates that the server's performance resides in the bottom 10% across all metrics, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 670.13 and a Passmark memory score of 671.42. The system exhibits low memory bandwidth, measuring 6.81 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Given these performance constraints, the A2m_v2 is appropriate for lightweight memory-intensive workloads, basic caching, or small-scale database operations that require a high memory footprint without demanding significant computational throughput.
og_description: Microsoft Azure A2m_v2 is an Av2-family virtual machine with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 20 GB local storage, offering a memory-optimized configuration with low-tier benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure A2m_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine with 2 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 20 GB of bundled local storage.
tagline: A low-cost, memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio for lightweight, memory-bound workloads with minimal processing requirements.
bullet_points: Features 2 dedicated Intel Xeon 8171M vCPUs running at 2.6 GHz
Provides 16.0 GB of system memory and 20 GB of bundled local storage
Delivers low-tier benchmark performance across compute and memory metrics
Operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor with x86_64 architecture
Appropriate for lightweight database operations and small-scale caching workloads
